title: International shipping 'chaos' as Strait of Hormuz closure sends ripple effects across the globe
description: Around 90% of global goods are transported by sea. And while just approximately 5% of that travels directly through the Strait of Hormuz, Iran's effective closure of the key passage is having a ripple effect, disrupting trade routes world wide. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Ebru Surucu-Balci, Assistant Professor in Circular Supply Chains at the School of Management at the University of Bradford.

title: Qatar orders departure of two Iranian diplomats over gas facility attack

title: Are US soldiers really "refusing orders" to fight in Iran war?

Also, Nigerian President Bola Tinubu meets King Charles and the royal family at Windsor Castle for a 2-day visit. And we learn more about "the right of salt" -- an age-old custom in North Africa honouring women during Ramadan.

title: Israel kills Iran’s intel chief as both sides ratchet up attacks on energy facilities
description: Iran suffered major blows on Wednesday with the killing of its intelligence minister, Esmail Khatib, in an Israeli strike and an attack on the South Pars natural gas field, one of the world's largest gas resources and a pillar of the country's energy supplies. Tehran vowed retaliation for the attacks, targeting facilities in Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, Kuwait, Bahrain and the UAE.

The US is easing sanctions on Venezuela, and suspending a century-old shipping law, in an effort to get more oil supplies into the global market. title: 'Acute risk' that Iraq could become once again victim of internal conflict as Iran war progresses

title: Iran did not try to rebuild enrichment after June 2025 attack, US intel chief says

FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

title: Kharkiv's opera and ballet theatre performances continue despite war
description: Ukraine’s second-largest city, Kharkiv, is just 30km from Russia and suffers almost daily bombardment from across the border. But as the years of war have gone by, the city’s cultural institutions have adapted in order to keep working despite the dangers. title: Generation Jihad | Day 12 of the Iran War
description:
After 12 days of war—and the death of Iran’s supreme leader—the Islamic Republic is under unprecedented pressure. Bill Roggio and Janatan Sayeh assess this initial phase of the war, the gap between Washington and Jerusalem’s goals, and the decisive question still looming over Tehran: will the Iranian people finish the job?

description:

title: 'Sex and the City' in Paris: Candace Bushnell on her upcoming one-woman show

title: What to know about the resignation of Joe Kent, counterterrorism chief and Trump loyalist
description: The head of the US's National Counterterrorism Center, Joe Kent, resigned from his post on Tuesday in opposition to the Trump administration's war on Iran. The former staunch Trump supporter said he could not support the war as "Iran posed no imminent threat" to the US.

title: To return to Gaza, Palestinian refugees first have to face Israeli-backed militias
description: Israel has said it is reopening the Rafah border crossing between the Gaza Strip and Egypt on Wednesday. Israeli authorities had partially opened the crossing on February 2 as part of the peace plan agreed with Hamas in October, but closed it on February 28 when Israeli and US missiles and planes began striking Iran.

title: Beirut building collapses amid fresh Israeli strikes in capital city's centre
description: #Israel led some of the most intense strikes against #Lebanon's capital centre since the start of the offensive on March 2. At least a dozen died, bringing up the number of people killed since early March to at least 900.

title: Trump pressures media on Iran war with claims of 'treason'
description: US President Donald Trump and his allies are attacking news organisations for their coverage of the war on Iran, a conflict the administration says is going well for the United States but is unpopular with the public and has plunged the Middle East into chaos. Trump accused unspecified “fake news media” of working with Tehran to spread AI-generated images and said they should be charged with treason.

title: Tens of millions more in acute hunger if Mideast war drags on, UN warns
description: An extra 45 million people could face acute hunger if the Mideast war carried on through June, swelling the number worldwide to a "terrible" high, the United Nations warned Tuesday. The war, now in its third week, has killed hundreds and seen Iran launch retaliatory strikes against Israel and Gulf nations, as well as a front opening in Lebanon with Israel battling Hezbollah.

title: Cuba vows 'unbreakable resistance' as US pressure mounts
description: Cuba's leader on Tuesday said the US would face "unbreakable resistance" if it tries to take over the impoverished island nation, as communist authorities scrambled to fix a nationwide electricity blackout. Cuba's government is under increasingly crushing pressure, with Washington enforcing an oil blockade and openly stating it wants to end the nearly seven-decade-old US standoff with the one-party communist state.

title: Zelensky on European tour to urge allies not to forget Ukraine
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Wednesday was making his visit to Spain after a stop in London on Tuesday seen as part of an effort by Kyiv to keep the war against Russia a priority for European capitals, even as they grapple with fall-out from the US-Israeli war against Iran that is consuming the Middle East.

title: Syrian refugees flee Israeli offensive in Lebanon, returning to a Syria in ruins
description: At the Joussieh border crossing near the Syrian city of Homs, thousands of Syrian refugees are fleeing the devastating war in Lebanon between Israel and Hezbollah. Driven out by Israeli air strikes after years of exile in Lebanon, families and children wait for hours before returning to a country where 90 percent of the population is surviving in post-Assad poverty. FRANCE 24's Dana Alboz and Hussam Hammoud report.

title: Former Columbia University president Lee C. Bollinger on the importance of free speech
description: The former president of Columbia University in New York, which was at the centre of sometimes violent protests against the war in Gaza, has spoken to FRANCE 24 about the importance of free speech and how universities have to teach it. Lee C. Bollinger is president emeritus of Columbia University; he ran it until just a year before the protests broke out. The university has long been known as a bastion of freedom of expression in the United States

description: Alexander Butyagin, an academic with Russias Hermitage Museum, is accused of illegal excavations in occupied Crimea.

title: European groups join aid convoy to Cuba amid crippling oil blockade
description: Millions have been plunged into darkness in Cuba as the USs total blockade has meant no fuel shipments in three months.

title: Why did Trump’s counterterrorism chief resign over Iran war?
description: US counterterrorism chief Joe Kent has resigned over Washington’s war on Iran, saying he cannot support the conflict.

title: Not just energy: How the Iran war could trigger a global food crisis
description: A critical fertiliser shortage threatens global food security amid Iran war.

title: Trump Signals He May Intervene in Cuba Following Iran and Venezuela
description: Still at war with Iran and in control in Venezuela, President Trump is signaling that he is about to intervene in another country.

title: Israel Has Killed Esmaeil Khatib, Iran’s Intelligence Minister
description: The Israeli military said on Wednesday that it had killed Esmaeil Khatib in an airstrike. Iran confirmed the killing.

title: Dozens Dead in Pakistani Airstrike on Kabul
description: The attack hit a drug rehabilitation facility, and Afghanistan vowed to retaliate, risking an escalation of the conflict between the two countries.

title: The Faroe Islands Are Changing Some of Europe’s Strictest Abortion Rules
description: A new law allowing abortion up to 12 weeks will be a major shift in an archipelago of 55,000 people, and there are strong feelings on both sides.

title: As Iran War Drags On, Europe Wants to Avoid a New Migration Crisis
description: A decade ago, a surge in migration to Europe spurred the far-right’s rise. European leaders now fear the Iran war could set off another crisis, and they have taken tentative steps to prepare.

title: Trump’s Friendship With Japan’s Leader Takaichi Faces Test Over Iran
description: In Washington on Thursday, President Trump is expected to press Sanae Takaichi for military help in the Strait of Hormuz. But she faces constraints on what she can offer.

title: He Lost a Leg for Russia. Then, He Says, His Country Betrayed Him.
description: Imprisoned for murder, Aleksandr Abbasov-Derskhan sought a new start in life and freedom by signing up to fight in Ukraine. But he says promised benefits proved illusory.

title: Netanyahu Hopes Strikes on Iran Will Lead to Uprising and Regime Change
description: Israeli attacks have targeted the command centers of Iran’s repressive, internal security forces in hopes that Iranians will overthrow their rulers. Some see that as wishful thinking.

title: Iran War Live Updates: Oil and Gas Prices Jump After Strike Hits Key Energy Site
description: The attack on South Pars, one of the world’s biggest gas fields, showed how the war threatens global energy supplies. Israel killed Iran’s intelligence minister, the latest high-ranking official to be targeted.

title: Arab Israelis share Jews’ distrust of Iran, but not their appetite for war
description:
Though most in the Arab community have little affinity for the Islamic Republic, few support the military campaign, seeing Tehran as less of a danger than the war's potential cost
The post Arab Israelis share Jews distrust of Iran, but not their appetite for war appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: 3,500 people evacuated, 10,946 damage claims filed since start of latest Iran war
description:
State comptroller report shows most evacuations from Tel Aviv-Jaffa, Beit Shemesh and Beersheba, with half of damage claims coming from Tel Aviv region
The post 3,500 people evacuated, 10,946 damage claims filed since start of latest Iran war appeared first on The Times of Israel.
